meta.note: Vocab from provided lists (5 core words per lesson as in app structure). Structure and sayings from teacher guide screenshots. Paradigms and prayers expanded as needed. Pronunciation rules shared with Prima Latina. Real clips integrated for puella/servus declensions + amo/video present conjugations (clip-first in paradigms, fallback to TTS). Old corpus sentences (greetings, daily life, inscriptions, short fables from Phaedrus I) introduced in practical_latin of L1, L2, L3, L4, L6, L7, L8, L9, L11, L12, L17, L22, L23+. Added 1-2 new vocab words per lesson as needed (e.g. animals like canis, lupus, vulpes, rana, leo, cervus from Phaedrus fables). Word order variations (swapping nom/acc subjects and objects while preserving meaning via cases) added to L3 and L15 from Phaedrus. Full details can be further expanded from book.

pronunciation_rules.condition: when followed by i and a vowel

prayer.title: Agnus Dei
prayer.latin_full: Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, miserere nobis. Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, miserere nobis. Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, dona nobis pacem.
prayer.english_full: Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, have mercy on us. Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, have mercy on us. Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, grant us peace.
prayer.new_portion_latin: Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, miserere nobis.
prayer.new_portion_english: Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, have mercy on us.
prayer.note: Each week learn the new portion and add to what you know.

prayer.latin_full: Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, miserere nobis. Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, miserere nobis. Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, dona nobis pacem.

prayer.title: Ave Maria
prayer.latin_full: Ave Maria, gratia plena, Dominus tecum. Benedicta tu in mulieribus, et benedictus fructus ventris tui, Iesus. Sancta Maria, Mater Dei, ora pro nobis peccatoribus, nunc et in hora mortis nostrae. Amen.
prayer.english_full: Hail Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with thee. Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb, Jesus. Holy Mary, Mother of God, pray for us sinners, now and at the hour of our death. Amen.
prayer.new_portion_latin: Ave Maria, gratia plena, Dominus tecum.
prayer.new_portion_english: Hail Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with thee.
prayer.note: Learn the opening of the Hail Mary.

prayer.title: Pater Noster
prayer.latin_full: Pater noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen tuum. Adveniat regnum tuum. Fiat voluntas tua, sicut in caelo et in terra. Panem nostrum quotidianum da nobis hodie, et dimitte nobis debita nostra, sicut et nos dimittimus debitoribus nostris. Et ne nos inducas in tentationem, sed libera nos a malo. Amen.
prayer.english_full: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done, on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread, and forgive us our trespasses, as we forgive those who trespass against us. And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. Amen.
prayer.new_portion_latin: Pater noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen tuum.
prayer.new_portion_english: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name.

ave-maria.title: Ave Maria (Hail Mary)
ave-maria.latin: Ave Maria, gratia plena, Dominus tecum. Benedicta tu in mulieribus, et benedictus fructus ventris tui, Iesus. Sancta Maria, Mater Dei, ora pro nobis peccatoribus, nunc et in hora mortis nostrae. Amen.
ave-maria.english: Hail Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with thee. Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb, Jesus. Holy Mary, Mother of God, pray for us sinners, now and at the hour of our death. Amen.

gloria-patri.title: Gloria Patri
gloria-patri.latin: Gloria Patri et Filio et Spiritui Sancto, sicut erat in principio et nunc et semper et in saecula saeculorum. Amen.
gloria-patri.english: Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen.

credo.title: Credo (Apostles' Creed — opening)
credo.latin: Credo in Deum, Patrem omnipotentem, Creatorem caeli et terrae. Et in Iesum Christum, Filium eius unicum, Dominum nostrum.
credo.english: I believe in God, the Father almighty, Creator of heaven and earth. And in Jesus Christ, his only Son, our Lord.

pater-noster.title: Pater Noster (Our Father)
pater-noster.latin: Pater noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen tuum. Adveniat regnum tuum. Fiat voluntas tua, sicut in caelo et in terra. Panem nostrum quotidianum da nobis hodie, et dimitte nobis debita nostra, sicut et nos dimittimus debitoribus nostris. Et ne nos inducas in tentationem, sed libera nos a malo. Amen.
pater-noster.english: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done, on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread, and forgive us our trespasses, as we forgive those who trespass against us. And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. Amen.
pater-noster.note: Second half learned across L21–25; full prayer complete at L25
